VerbalDescription: Same grid size as input. On black background, you will see an object that has a gap in it. Fill in the gap in the object with the same color boxes as you see in the rest of the object. Complete the pattern so that each side is symmetrical and looks like the other side.
Copy the grid. Add a box so that the feature is complete and symmetrical.
Copy the image so it is the same size and shape.  Mirror the image so that any black squares are filled in and the shape becomes symmetrical in size and colour.

Grid size remains the same as the test input. All coloured grids in the test input remain on the same position. Check the pattern of arrangement of test input grid colours and fill  the colour at the missing point. The missing point can be at any part along the coloured grid
Keep same grid size. Complete the image so it's a mirror image all round. Keep same colours
Complete the pattern shown on the opposite side of the shape to make the shape full
Copy the pattern and dimensions and fill in the missing cell to complete the symetrical pattern using the correct colour to make it symetrical with the rest of the pattern.
Copy from the input, the rule will fill in any blocks that are missing that prevent the shape being symmetrical, so here you just add the extra red block on the right to get symmetry.

Fill in the hole so that it is one continuous shape. If the hole is in the center of the shape then just fill that in. If the hole is on the outside edge of the shape only close the shape and leave the center hollow.
You need to copy the shape across to the answer screen. once you have done this, colour in the two missing square to complete the shape with the matching colour. then submit.
The solution is to fill in any gaps along rows and columns and create a complete shape. Replicate the grid from the input and fill in the 2 boxes within it with green
Reside the grid to the size of the task demonstration. Select the edit button and the correct corresponding colour. The rule is to fill in the gap in alignment with the pattern presented.
Use the same grid size. Copy the object from the input. Fill the missing the grids with colour major colour of the object.

Look at the input grid, there will be a shape with a gap or missing colour blocks. The answer is the same grid size as the input, the shape will be in the same location as the input with the same colours and shape. You will need to fill in the gap with the correct colour. If it is a shape that only has a border and no inner colour, you will need to complete the border following the same colour (if all one colour) or pattern (if there are multiple colours). If the shape is filled and has a couple of missing blocks, your shape will be the same as the input but with these blocks also filled in.
shape should be symmetric. filled in the necessary black squares to complete the pattern. pattern should be the outer layer of a square or rectangle. don't need to filled the center black squares.
Copy the input grid in the same size and color. Make the colored shape bilaterally symmetrical by adding the minimum number of colors and cells to create that symmetry.
finish the pattern, which in this case was six orangey red blocks in the bottom right corner
Keep the same grid size as the input. There will be a clearly identifiable pattern in the input with a gap in it. Your answer will be to recreate the pattern with the gap filled it.

To do this task you need a little bit of judgment. I copy the input and then i look at the object. I will then look where the object is not symetrical and i will add the colours to make it symetrical.
Copy the input grid. Repair the shape until it becomes a symetrical pattern (use corrosponding colours, dependent on the pattern).
Fill in the spaces to complete the pattern
Fill in the missing blocks in the pyramid, but do not fill in the rectangle near the top of the pyramid. Keep size and color.
Use the same sized grid as the input. Replicate the shape using the same colour. Fill in the two missing squares along the longest length of the object in the same colour.
Use a same size 12x12 grid. Copy the red shape over in the same spot. Fill in the two missing pieces on the upper left edge red to complete the shape.
Fill in the 2 red blanks down the long side to the left but keep the black hole in the middle. 12x12 grid and what not
Use the same grid size as the input. There will be one object in the input that is not coloured, in your answer, match the colour of the grid square( keep its colour the same).

Copy the input to the output. There will be some shape such that one of its flat edges is made NOT flat by two to three squares of black missing from the edge. Fill these black squares with the color of the shape such that it has a flat edge.

Copy the grid and cells exactly. There will be some cells along the longest edge of the shape that are black.  Fill in the cells to match the colour of the main object and complete the longest edge of the shape.
Using the same grid size and color, copy the shape exactly. Fill in the gap to make the shape symmetrical. If there is a symmetrical gap you don't need to fill this in.

Copy the size and objects from the input grid. If there is just one large object that has gaps in its shape, fill the gaps with the same coloured blocks. Do not do this if the gap is within the object itself
The shape is completed along the longest side.
Fill the shape apart from the rectangle in the middle
Use the same grid size as in the input. Select copy input. Fill in the missing spaces on the right side of the object with the matching color of the object
Fill in the missing cells at the longer base of the shape only, NOT the ones on the inside, with the same color. Keep the same grid size.
Use the same grid size as the input. There will be one or two empty object in the input, in your answer, Fill the colour of the objects ( keep its colour the same).
copy input grid. Fix the 'broken' section on the edge of the shape.
Fill in the missing pieces that are found on the edges of the original shapes. Keep them the same color.
Using the same 14x14 grid. Copy the whole yellow shape except fill in the three missing pieces of the yellow shape yellow (top right).
Same grid size, shape and colour. Fill in the gaps in the shape outline but not the middle section
Fill in the squares that make the shape incomplete - apart from the rectangle in the middle

For this solution fill in any blocks missing from the EDGE of the shape. 
Other missing block can be missing if they are surrounded by other blocks in the shape.
keep the grid dimensions as 14 by 14 fill in the missing squares on the edge of the shape with the same colour which in the case is yellow and leave the squares that are blank in the middle unfilled.
Finish the lines with their respective vertical and horizontal colors. The intersecting block is the color of the horizontal line.

Copy the grid size. The picture should show coloured blocks in an arrangement that resembles two dashed coloured lines. Fill these dashed lines in the same colour, to create two coloured lines that reach from side to side and bottom to top. The horizontal line will go over the top of the vertical line, so the horizontal line should be a solid line of colour, breaking up the vertical line.

Copy the input grid size and design. First, complete the horizontal set of blocks so it makes a complete line in the same colour as the blocks already there. Next, complete the vertical line with the same colour as the vertical blocks, but do not recolour the intersection. The intersection colour needs to remain the same as the horizonal line.

Use the same grid size as the input. Complete the horizontal coloured lines by filling in any missing squares with their respective colour. Do the same for the vertical bars with exception of where it intersects with the horizontal bar.
good correction

Match the grid size to the original at 6 by 6. Fill in the black spaces of the horizontal and vertical lines with the correct that are current used. Make sure the overlapping square has the horizontal line on top of the vertical line.

duplicate the lines into a new grid of the same size. Fill in all blank spaces to complete solid lines. Where the lines cross, make the color of the intersection the same color as the horizontal line.

You must copy the size of the demonstration grid.  There will be a vertical line that is missing colors and a horizontal line that has black spaces.  You must color the horizontal line in so that there are no dark spaces.  The entire line must be the same color as the original (not black).  For the vertical line you will have it in the same position.  You must color in any black squares with the same color as the vertical line.  Do not color in the horizontal line square, the horizontal line will be all one color.  The vertical line will have a fully colored line above and beneath the horizontal line.
You need to connect the disconnected colored squares with the like color, and the horizontal line color goes on top of the vertical line color.

With the same grid size, copy the pattern. Then, fill in the gaps using the same colors to generate 2 perpendicular lines that cross each other, and reach the length of the grid. The horizontal line appears on top of the vertical line.
Use the same size grid as original, fill in the gaps in each line in the same colour. Horizontal lines appear above vertical lines so that you see the whole of the horizontal line in the final image
Copy from input. The end result needs to be two lines overlapping. Think of what you start with as two lines with gaps in between them. Fill these gaps with the neighbouring colour. Grid is 10x10
Fill in the gaps. Start with the vertical line and then the horizontal line. Horizontal line will appear to go over the top.
Connect all the broken "dotted" line. The final object after connected will look like one straight line without disturbance.

Copy the grid size. Then, fill in the black spaces in between the coloured squares to create two coloured lines. IF the colours are different, then the horizontal line will always go through the vertical line, so the horizontal line will be the same colour
Use the same size grid as in the input. Use the same colours as well. Fill in both the row that has the pink squares in and the column with the pink squares in pink.

There should be 2 dotted lines one horizontal and one vertical. Fill in the lines with the colour used for each respective line. If the lines are different colours make sure at the intersection that the horizontal lines colour is used
Keep the grid size the same. Keep the color the same. Fill in the missing square with the same color to make a full row. The horizonal line should all be the same color.
Use the same size grid. Transpose the original image but instead making the dashed lines solid lines stretching from edge to edge with the horizontal line overlapping the vertical line.
adjust your grid to the size of the first and topmost color,and fill it with the same color

hit the copy input button
complete the edge of the rectangle with the same color as the input
There will be an incomplete coloured rectangular outline, with many holes in it. WIth the same colours and grid dimensions, recreate the rectangular outline without these holes. Full lines.
Keep the color and grid the same. Fill in the missing blocks with the same color to create a object. This could be a rectangle or square.
Using the same size grid as the input, complete any missings blocks to form an unbroken outline of a shape (using the same colour as the outline blocks already present)
Copy the pattern and cell dimensions from the example and simply fill in all the black squares that make the border / sqaure broken. you will end up with a one square deep unbroken border.
Copy the grid size, draw the outline of the shape, filling in any blank spaces to that there is a fully solid outline with no gaps
Copy from input and then finish the rectangle sides in the same colour as the shape, (orange), DO NOT fill in the middle of the shape, keep it black.
Using the shape on the left, use that same colour to complete the edges so you have a continuous edge to your rectangle
you would copy from input and make a square out of what is there.or a rectangle, so 4 straight lines that are all connected into a box shape.
In the example, you will see the makings of a shape. Copy the grid from the example and finish the shape without changing size, color, or location.
Make the grid the same size as the original grid and connect the blocks to make a rectangle using the same colors.
Keep the grid the same size.  Fill in the gaps of the rectangle using the same colour throughout.

Resize the grid to the same grid size the input is given on. Then, place squares with the same colour at the vertices of the corners of the incomplete shape presented on the output grid. Then, make a complete line to make either a square or rectangle.
Copy from input and fill in the missing black squares to create a one square wide frame of the same color . Just fill it in
all square type same square box

Copy the grid size and image from the original keeping the same colour scheme. The original pattern is incomplete. Complete the rectangular pattern filling in the missing areas to complete the rectangle.
Set grid to 11 by 16. Move object into the same positions in the new grid using the same color. Connect the dots to create a rectangle. Do not fill the inside of the rectangle.
just copy input and fill out the rest of the lines so it finishes creating the shape
change the grid size to 11 by 14. then complete the shape that's outlined with the same colour blocks

The input will contain a bunch of dots that form a very rough outline of a rectangular shape. Fill in the edges of this rectangular shape with the same color of the dots, such that all of the dots are connected on the edges of this rectangular shape. Your output will be the same size as your input.

Use the same size grid as the input.. In your answers, at every intersection where blocks overlap, the vertical color block must overlap the horizontal color block. Keep all colors the same except the overlapping block which must have the same color as the vertical blocks

Resize the grid to the same size as the image. On the top and bottom rows fill all spaces inside of the two vertical lines with the same color as the vertical lines. Do not transfer the vertical lines to the new image.
copy the example shape and grid over from the input and the add in coloured blocks to complete the remaining two sides of the rectangle then delete the original two lines from the input
Resize the grid to the same size as input, draw lines that, when added to the input shown, would form a rectangle. Use the same colour as shown.

You will see two colored lines. Copy the grid as show in the example. Then color the points in the grid that would turn the two lines into a square or rectangle and then get rid of the original lines, leaving you with only the two new lines, same color as the original.

There are two parallel lines in the input box. Create two new lines of the same color by filling in the blank boxes between the outermost squares of the two lines. Then turn all of the original colored lines black.

Resize to fit the input grid size.  Then replicate the entire image on your grid using the same colors.  Next you want to make each L shape into a 2X2 square by filling in the missing square with the opposite square color that is shown on the grid.  So if the first L shape has 3 yellow squares filled in and then the other color on the grid is green you want to make that extra square green.  You should have 4 squares that are 2X2 when you're done.  Each 2X2 should have 3 squares that are the same color (i.e. yellow) and the fourth square should be the other color that's on the input grid.

Use the same grid size as in the input. There will be "L" shaped objects that are either green or yellow. Simply, add an additional square to those "L" shapes objects creating a square object using the other color. So, if there's is a Yellow L shape object, add a green square. If there is a Green L shape object, add a yellow square.

Using the same grid size as the sample, reproduce the grids in the same colors as the sample. Then turn each set of three-celled yellow-colored shapes into a square by adding a green cell in the empty cell in order to complete the square. Also turn each set of three-celled green-colored shapes into a square by adding a yellow cell in the adjacent empty cell in order to complete the square.
Replicate the input (same grid size, same objects). Extend these objects to squares using a different colour, specifically: if the object is green, use yellow; if the object is yellow, use green.
Using the same grid size, fill in the gaps of these L shapes to create 2x2 squares. Use green to fill in the gap of the yellow Ls and use yellow to fill in the gap of the green Ls.

The rule is that you are going to make all 4 objects that were originally on the grid into square shapes (2x2), using the opposite color (so if the 3 original squares were green, you are going to use a yellow square to complete that square shape and if the 3 original squares were yellow, use a green square to complete it). You should have 4 multi-colored squares when complete.

You should see various green and yellow L shapes in the input. Use the same grid size as the input. You should copy over each of these L shapes exactly, and then fill them in to make squares with the OPPOSITE colour - for example, if you have a green L, you should fill the remaining square in yellow so that you have a cube shape. Do this for each of the L shapes.
LANDSCAPPING COLOR CHANGE
Copy from input and then use the yellow square to make blocks.
Keep grid and colors the same . Duplicate shapes on the left . After that fill in with a YELLOW cube to create a box . you should have multiple green and yellow SQUARE boxes .
Copy from input and then fill in the corner of each green shape yellow, thus making multiple green squares that have 1 yellow corner.

On a 10 x 10 board, you're presented with L shaped quadrants in green and yellow. The rule to solve this board is to fill in the missing space to make these shapes square using the opposite color. I.E. 3 greens and 1 yellow in a 2 x 2 square or 1 green and 3 yellows in a 2 x 2 square
Complete each square with a block of the opposing color.

Resize to the same size as the image. Transfer the image to the new image grid. Use green on black spots next to the yellow that makes the yellow plus green into a 2 by 2 square. There should be one green spot per every 2 by 2 square.
Complete the squares by using a yellow or green color, which ever is the opposite of the first 3, to make a 2x2 square.
Same grid. Choose green and complete the squares by adding the fourth piece for each.

Keep the grid size the same. transfer over the same colours and shapes as in the test grid and then add opposite colours (green if shape is yellow, yellow if shape is green to create coloured 2x2 squares
I USED THE SAME INPUT GRID AND LATER I CHANGED THE SIZE OF THE SQUARE

Adjust the grid to six by six. There will be two uneven shapes in different colors. Keep their starting locations the same. Extend the colors so that the shortest part goes exactly the same distance as the longest part.
Fill in the shapes so they become full rectangles or squares

Copy test grid, and in order to turn each of the shapes on the grid into a quadrilateral, highlight the cells next to each shape which need changing to achieve this and change their colours to that of the colours of their respective shapes.

Copy from input so that the grid matches the input (6x6). The shapes fill to make 2 four-sided objects in this task. So fill each object with their corresponding colors so they make squares or rectangle shapes.
Keep grid and colors the same. Square the shapes off . Fill in the missin colors until you have created two rectangles

The rule is that you should change the shapes to rectangles so for example the blue L hsape should be transformed into a blue rectangle, occupying the same position on the grid. Resize the grid to 6 x 6, then copy input to the grid using the copy input button. You should then use the edit buttion to select the colour of each L shape in turn, and fill in the gaps to make them into rectangles.
Turn your shape into a rectangle of solid colour using its current width and height as your guide
Each image contains incomplete rectangle shapes. Copy the grid over, and complete the rectangle with its corresponding colors. If it's a blue "L" shape, use blue to complete the rectangle.
Copy the input, then fill in the shapes to become full squares or rectangles.
Same grid size. Replicate the objects seen in the input, in the same positions. Extend them to full rectangles and fill them in with their respective colours.
Copy over the grid and shapes. Add additional squares to turn the shapes into a four sided shape of the same colour. Use the least number of additional coloured squares as possible.
Same grid size, and colours but complete figures to form rectangles or squares
Complete the puzzles by completing the squares or rectangles.

Use the same dimensions as the demonstration/input. Objects in the solution must be complete squares/rectangles. Identify the objects in the input, and note that some grid squares need to be filled in using the same color as the original object to make it a square/rectangle. Without moving objects left/right, up/down, create exact copies of the original object but now as a completed square/rectangle. This will require you to "add on" colored squares to the original object to complete it. Do not add any grid squares that make the new square/rectangle longer or wider than the original object's longest and widest edge. Do not remove any squares from the original object - you are only adding grid squares to make the object a square/rectangle.
Keeping the same colour and grid size. Fill in the missing gaps to make the shape have 4 sides.

Make the grid size 10x10.  Then take the smallest connected group of one color and just copy that over (use the same color) in the same position in the 10x10 grid.  And then fill in only what the dimensions are.  So if the dimensions as a whole are 3x3, then fill in 3x3.
Use the same grid size as in the input. Fill empty spaces of object horizontally and vertically with same color as object.
Copy grid from square and fill in the black portions of each object.

Using the same size grid as the first image, recreate the first image exactly, and then extend each of the shapes to form rectangles/squares. Round off these shapes at the furthest point they extend. Use the same colors for the corresponding shapes.
Copy test input. if any objects are unusually shaped, make them into squares/rectangles, using the same colours.
Keep cell size the same. Keep the colours the same as previous shapes. Fill in the shapes, so they become either squares or rectangles. As if you're filling in the missing pieces.

use the same size grid as the input grid. fill in the "gaps" in the shape/shapes in the input grid to make a rectangle/square (depending on how the original shape was) use the same colour as the rest of the shape
resize grid to 10x10 and fill entire grid in pink.

Keep grid size the same as input. Keep object colours the same and in the same position. Colour the squares in the same colour to transform the irregularly shaped object to either a square or rectangle
just copy the task and edit the color and fill the all boxes
Fill in the holes to make a square of the same color.
There will be a pixelated shape in the input. Using the same size grid and colour, fill in the gaps of the input to make a square
Resize the grid to match the input size. The misses colours need to be filled in to match the input and create a square, leaving a border of black around the outside
Change the grid size to the same as the one shown.  Create a square of the same colour and size as the one shown by filling in the black squares.  Do not move the shape.
In this task i copy the input exactly and then fill in the gaps of the shape given to turn it into a square which is no bigger than the height and width of the original shape.

Use the same grid size as the size of the biggest object in the input. In your answer, the object should be added extra blocks to make it a solid square object while maintaining the original max length and width of the input block. Keep all colors same
You will see an incomplete square. Keeping the colors and grid size the same, fill in the missing parts of the square.
Take the outline of the pattern and use the same color blocks to fill in all the black spots. make it fully solid of the same color.

Make the new grid the same size as the original. Using the same colors as the original, make a block that is filled in from each side and corner, one solid block, the same color as the shape in the original.. from the furthest left and right edges and top and bottom edges.

Copy the grid size. The picture should show a coloured shape that resembles a solid coloured square or rectangle with black blocks in an irregular pattern over the top. Remove these black blocks to create a solid coloured square or rectangle with a black border around it.
Copy the grid exactly. Copy the target exactly. Then fill in the smallest square possible that contains the target image. Use the same colour as the target image.
grid remains same, blocks filled to make a square

Choose 'Copy from input' button. choose 'Select' and draw a box that contains the boundaries of the shape (but no further). Click on the color of the original shape. Click to the right of the grid to verify that the resulting box is the same color.

Resize grid to the same size as the image. Transfer blue blocks to the same locations as in the image. In the new grid, use the blue color to complete the original object into a complete square shape. It should be a 4 by 4 square.
Same grid size, fill in the black boxes to make a square from the existing colored boxes

Use the same size grid as the input grid. The object in the upper right is the "sample" object. Do not include this object in your drawing. Using the same colors as the two objects to the left, make them look exactly like the sample object by filling them in.
Create a copy of the grey shape in the top right. Use the respective colors of the other unfinished shapes. Then remove the grey sample shape

There are three colors in your initial grid. One gray object in the upper right hand corner, and a few squares of each of two other colors. You are going to recreate the grid exactly, then recreate the shape of the gray object where those colored squares are, but in those colors, not gray. You should have three identically sized and shaped objects, but in three different colors. Then turn the gray one black.
Copy input from grid. Use the select tool to copy the grey shape so that the two other coloured shapes are the same. Then use the edit tool to remove the grey shape.

Resize the grid to 10x10. There are multiple items in the input box. One item is gray. That item will not be reproduced, its purpose is to show the shape and size of the other items. The other items have some squares in the correct colors already filled in. There is only one way that the gray shape can be filled in using these predefined color squares. Using the same color as the predefined squares, complete the shape shown by the gray item.

Identify the largest object.  Now, where there are seemingly random cells, add cells of the same colour to create copies of the large object.  Once the copies are created, change the cells of the original large object to black.
take the shape which is grey and remove this however with the colours are left make the shape of them the same as the orignial grey shape.

Use the same grid size as the input. There will be one grey object, and two incomplete versions of the same shape in the input. In your answer, copy and finish the incomplete shapes using their existing colors, keeping them in the same position in the grid. Do not copy the grey object.

First recreate the grid input size And place the all coloured blocks as per the input grid except for the shape in the top right hand of the grid. It is this shape you will be recreating using the blocks already placed as a reference. Match the colour of the new shapes you draw to the blocks in the input grid. And start the top of the shapes using the blocks provided.

Copy from input, You are given a full shape as an example of what shapes you will be making. You are also given reference points for said shape. Using the correct corresponding colours of each reference point, recreate the shape given twice, each one should be a different colour. Use the edit option to do this. Then use the edit option to 'erase' the reference shape (fill it in with background colour, black)

you just fill in the missing piece to make a full shape

Fill up the object by applying/filling the same object colour to the only one black grid that appeared within the object.

use exact same grid with the shapes in the same spots, then fill in any hole or break in the shape with the same color

Use the same grid size as in the input. There will be one square that has not been completed. Please complete the object. and keep it's color the same
Use the same size grid as original, recreate the shape in the same size and colour, any breaks in a contiguous shape and colour are filled in to create a shape without gaps
